title: Dimorphotheca pluvialis
taxonomy: Dimorphotheca pluvialis, also known as the stormers ear or cape marigold, belongs to the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the order Asterales. Further, it is a genus within the tribe Arctotideae. Dimorphotheca pluvialis is native to South Africa, where it grows as an annual or short-lived perennial. Its classification is also sometimes referred to as a synonym of the genus Arcturus.

intro: Dimorphotheca pluvialis, also known as the Cape marigold, is a flowering plant species native to southern Africa, characterized by its bright orange or yellow flowers and annual growth habit. The health benefits of Dimorphotheca pluvialis include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues.
